steps_en: Draw a row of inward-facing half-moons along the edge.
Fill each half-moon solid black.
Draw a thin aura outside each black bump, following its curve.
Continue with a second, third aura outward until area is filled.
steps_zh: 沿著區塊邊緣畫一排朝內的半月形（小弧凸起）。
把每個半月填滿黑色。
在每個黑色凸起外側畫一條跟著弧度走的細線——這是第一道光環。
繼續往外畫第二道、第三道光環，直到填滿區塊。
why_important_en: Crescent Moon teaches the foundational concept of the 'aura' — a line that follows and offsets the contour of a previous shape. This technique reappears in dozens of other tangles.
why_important_zh: Crescent Moon 教會「光環」這個基礎概念——一條線跟著前一個形狀的輪廓向外回應——這個技法之後會出現在數十個其他圖樣裡。

creator_role: Co-founder of Zentangle (named after daughter Molly Hollibaugh)
steps_en: Draw a long ribbon (two parallel curves with rounded ends) across the area.
Draw a second ribbon that appears to pass behind the first — when the line touches the first ribbon, lift the pen and continue on the other side.
Continue adding ribbons; each passes behind the previous.
Fill the gaps between ribbons with solid black.
steps_zh: 畫一條長帶（兩條平行曲線、兩端為圓形），橫越區塊。
再畫一條帶子，看起來像「從第一條後面穿過去」——當線觸到第一條時，提筆，到另一邊再繼續。
持續加入帶子，每一條都從前面那條的後方穿過。
條與條之間的縫隙填黑。
why_important_en: Hollibaugh teaches the 'draw behind' technique — the most reliable way to create the illusion of three dimensions, like the layered weave of a bamboo basket.
why_important_zh: Hollibaugh 教會「畫在後面」這個技法——是製造「立體錯覺」最可靠的方式，就像竹編籃子的層次感。

first_published_in: Original Zentangle deck; One Zentangle a Day Day 1
steps_en: Start with a small dot or tight inner curl.
Spiral outward with continuous curves, keeping spacing even.
End with a small hook or curve.
Pack many spirals tightly, leaving small triangular gaps.
steps_zh: 從一個小點或緊密的小捲開始。
用連續的曲線往外旋轉，每一圈間距盡量一致。
結尾以一個小勾或小弧收住。
把許多旋紋緊密排列，留下小三角形空隙。
why_important_en: Printemps is one of the most calming tangles — the repetitive spiral rhythm is like rolling beads in your hand, deeply meditative.
why_important_zh: Printemps 是所有圖樣中最讓人冷靜的——重複的旋轉節奏像是手心搓念珠，極具冥想效果。

steps_en: Lightly dot a grid in the area.
Connect each pair of adjacent dots with an outward-curving arc — first all horizontals, then all verticals.
The result forms a network of concave-edged diamonds.
Add a small circle, dot, or solid black center to each diamond.
steps_zh: 在區塊內輕點一格網狀的點。
用「向外凸的弧線」連接每對相鄰的點——先全部水平方向、再全部垂直方向。
結果形成一張「凹邊菱形」的網絡。
在每個菱形中畫一個小圓、一個點，或填一塊黑心。
why_important_en: Cadent demonstrates how a 'hidden grid' can bring order without rigidity.
why_important_zh: Cadent 示範了「隱藏的網格」如何帶來秩序而不顯得僵硬。

creator_role: Co-founder of Zentangle (inspired by Alphonse Mucha)
steps_en: Start with a long, gentle S-curve as the main stem.
Extend teardrop-shaped petals from the stem that curl back toward it.
Draw each petal with a continuous lasso stroke — out from the stem and back to it.
Add smaller secondary petals inside the main petals for depth.
steps_zh: 從一條柔順的長 S 曲線開始，作為主莖。
從莖上延伸出水滴形的瓣，往莖的方向回捲。
每片瓣以連續的「套索」筆法畫成——從莖出發、繞回莖。
在主瓣裡再加更小的次級瓣，營造層次。
why_important_en: Mooka is the 'organic representative' of Zentangle — a flowing counterpoint to grid-based tangles, like peony branches in an ink painting.
why_important_zh: Mooka 是禪繞中的「有機代表」——與規則網格圖樣完全相反的流動感，像水墨畫裡的牡丹枝條。

first_published_in: Early Zentangle deck; One Zentangle a Day
steps_en: Lay down a grid of dots.
Connect each dot diagonally to form a grid of standing diamonds.
At each intersection of four diamond corners, place a small black diamond or filled dot.
Optionally add a small dot at the center of each large diamond.
steps_zh: 鋪一格網狀的點。
把每個點對角連接，形成「立著的小方塊」（菱形）格網。
四個菱形角相交處，放一個小黑菱或填黑點。
視需要在每個大菱形中心點一個小點。
why_important_en: Florz is the simplest grid tangle — the first step into a world of repeating rhythm, reminiscent of Taipei's old terrazzo floor tiles.
why_important_zh: Florz 是最簡單的網格圖樣，是進入「重複韻律」世界的第一步——像台北老房子的磨石子地磚。

creator_role: Co-founders of Zentangle (named after an Italian pasta dumpling)
steps_en: Loosely sketch irregular horizontal and vertical lines, dividing the area into cells.
In each cell, draw a plump 'pillow' shape that almost — but not quite — touches all four edges.
The thin gaps between pillows form a soft pebble-like grid.
Optionally shade one side of each pillow.
steps_zh: 鬆鬆地畫一些不太規則的水平與垂直線條，分成許多格子。
在每個格子裡，畫一個飽滿的「枕頭形」——幾乎、但不完全頂到四個邊。
枕頭間的細縫形成柔軟的鵝卵石網格。
視需要在枕頭某一側做陰影。
why_important_en: Like a steamer of just-cooked dumplings — the pillow shapes give 'Nzeppel a tactile, layered quality even without explicit shading.
why_important_zh: 像一籠剛蒸好的小餃子——枕頭給 'Nzeppel 一種無需明顯陰影就能感覺到的觸覺與層次。

steps_en: Fill the area with circles of varying sizes.
Place the largest circles first, fill gaps with medium circles, then fill smaller gaps with the smallest circles.
Optionally add a small shadow on one side of each circle to make it look like a bubble.
steps_zh: 用大小不一的圓填滿區塊。
先放最大的圓，讓中等的填補空隙，最小的填空隙的空隙。
視需要在每個圓的某一側加陰影，讓它看起來像泡泡。
why_important_en: Tipple is the universal 'filler' — when an area needs gentle texture without competing with the main pattern, like a small bamboo coaster under a teacup.
why_important_zh: Tipple 是萬用「填料」——當區塊需要溫和質地、又不想搶走主圖樣時就用它，像茶碗下的小竹墊。

steps_en: Draw a small Y or three-line star.
Add fourth and fifth rays to form a small radial starburst.
Scatter starbursts across the area, varying their size.
Add small dots between starbursts as breathing space.
steps_zh: 畫一個小「Y」字，或三線星形。
加上第四、第五條光線，形成小型放射星芒。
把這種星芒散布在區塊各處，大小變化。
在星芒之間點上小點，作為呼吸空間。
why_important_en: Ahh is like a small sigh on paper — the name 'Ahh' is the sound of a relaxed exhale.
why_important_zh: Ahh 像是紙上的小聲嘆息——名字「啊」就是放鬆的吐氣聲。

steps_en: Lay a square grid.
In each cell, draw two outward-curving arcs from opposite corners — they meet in the middle to form a four-petal pillow.
Arcs in adjacent cells naturally connect; the whole reads as a quilt of soft pillows.
Add a dot or shadow at the center of each petal.
steps_zh: 鋪一格方格網。
每個方格內，從相對的兩個角向外畫兩條外凸的弧——它們在中央相遇，形成一個四瓣枕頭。
相鄰方格的弧自然連接，整體像鋪滿柔軟枕頭的綢被。
在花瓣中心加點或陰影。
why_important_en: Bales is one of the gentlest grid tangles; the curves wash away all the rigidity of the underlying grid.
why_important_zh: Bales 是最溫柔的網格圖樣之一；曲線把網格的剛硬感全洗掉了。

steps_en: Draw a vertical line.
Shoot a row of zigzag lightning from the line.
Draw a second vertical line; its zigzags face the first set, like meshing gears.
Continue with alternating columns; optionally fill inner triangles black.
steps_zh: 畫一條垂直線。
從線上射出一排鋸齒狀的閃電。
再畫第二條垂直線；從它射出的鋸齒朝向第一組，像齒輪互咬。
持續以交替欄位填滿；視需要把內三角填黑。
why_important_en: Static brings high-contrast, energetic patterns — perfect for visual impact.
why_important_zh: Static 帶來高對比、能量感的圖樣——適合製造視覺衝擊。

steps_en: Draw a row of adjacent circles along a baseline.
Draw a second row of circles offset by half a circle from the first.
Draw a thin aura inside each circle.
Fill the small triangular intersections with black.
steps_zh: 沿基線畫一排相鄰的圓。
上方再畫一排相鄰的圓，與下排錯開半個圓。
在每個圓的內側畫一道細光環。
三個圓交會的小縫處填黑。
why_important_en: Onamato shows that 'inside auras' (auras drawn inward) can also create three-dimensional depth.
why_important_zh: Onamato 示範了「光環往內畫」也能創造立體感。

steps_en: Draw a square grid.
Fill alternating squares solid black, like a chessboard.
Optionally rotate the grid 45° to create a diamond checkerboard.
steps_zh: 畫一格方格網。
把交錯的方格填黑，像棋盤。
視需要把方格旋轉 45° 形成菱形棋盤。
why_important_en: Knightsbridge is the textbook 'anchor tangle' — a pure black-and-white area that makes adjacent tangles look more refined by contrast.
why_important_zh: Knightsbridge 是教科書級的「定錨圖樣」——一塊純黑白對比區，會讓旁邊的圖樣看起來更精緻。

steps_en: Draw a row of upward-pointing triangles along a baseline.
Above, draw another row of upward triangles, positioned between the points of the row below.
Continue stacking upward to form a triangular mosaic.
Fill alternating triangles with black.
steps_zh: 沿基線畫一排朝上的三角形。
上方再畫一排朝上的三角形，位於下排的兩個尖點之間。
持續往上堆疊，形成三角的鑲嵌結構。
把交替的三角形填黑。
why_important_en: Sez is one of the fastest ways to create a 'woven/scaled' illusion.
why_important_zh: Sez 是最快創造「織紋／鱗片」錯覺的方法之一。

steps_en: Draw a curving stem.
Grow plump balloon-shaped leaves along the stem.
Each leaf has a small notch where it joins the stem.
Add fine veins or one-sided shading.
steps_zh: 畫一條彎彎的莖。
在莖上長出圓鼓鼓的氣球狀葉。
每片葉與莖相接處有一個小凹口。
加細葉脈或單側陰影。
why_important_en: Pokeleaf is the classic 'vine/round leaf' organic tangle, often used in borders.
why_important_zh: Pokeleaf 是經典的「藤蔓／圓葉」有機圖樣，常用於邊框。

steps_en: Draw a hexagonal or rounded honeycomb cell.
Inside the cell, draw a curving 'flame' shape.
Add another cell next to or above it; the flame faces the previous cell.
Fill surroundings with black or hatching.
steps_zh: 畫一個六角或圓潤的蜂巢格。
格內畫一個彎曲的「火焰」形。
上面或旁邊再畫一格，火焰朝向前一格。
周圍填黑或畫斜線。
why_important_en: Beelight bridges geometric and organic — a hybrid that opens new possibilities.
why_important_zh: Beelight 是「植物與幾何」之間的橋樑。

steps_en: From a baseline, draw a thin J-shaped curve ending with a solid dot.
Draw another J-curve next to it, similar in direction but a slightly different angle.
Continue along the area; the whole resembles wind-blown grass.
The dots at the tips are signature.
steps_zh: 從基線畫一條細的 J 字曲線，尾端加一個實心圓點。
旁邊再畫一條 J 字曲線，方向類似但角度略不同。
沿區塊持續畫，整體像隨風起伏的草。
尖端的小點是它的特徵。
why_important_en: Fescu works as a border or filler, contrasting nicely with heavier neighbors.
why_important_zh: Fescu 適合做邊界或填料，與較重的鄰居形成對比。

creator_role: Co-founder of Zentangle (named after a place)
steps_en: Draw a wavy horizontal line across the area.
Below the wave, draw a series of vertical 'skirt' arcs.
Below the arcs, draw another wavy line that echoes the first.
Continue stacking; fill arc interiors with black.
steps_zh: 在區塊上畫一條波浪狀水平線。
從波浪向下畫一連串如「裙擺」般的垂直弧。
在弧下方再畫一條波浪線，與第一條相呼應。
持續往下堆疊；弧的內部填黑。
why_important_en: Shattuck offers a strong horizontal rhythm — visually like a row of suspended black fruit.
why_important_zh: Shattuck 提供強烈的水平節奏——視覺像一排懸吊的黑色果實。

steps_en: Draw a comma-like curving stem.
Add a small swirl or curl at one end.
Add parallel diagonal lines or small leaves along the stem.
Scatter many Zingers in different directions.
steps_zh: 畫一條像逗號的彎莖。
一端加上一個小旋或小捲。
在莖上加一些平行斜線或小葉。
在區塊內隨意散布許多 Zinger，方向各異。
why_important_en: Zinger is technically a 'decorative element' rather than a fill tangle — a sprinkle of flourish.
why_important_zh: Zinger 嚴格說是「裝飾元素」——是錦上添花的小花俏。

steps_en: Start with a triangle (or any near-regular polygon).
From each vertex, draw a straight line to a point on the adjacent edge — but stop before reaching the edge, then continue inward.
Each new line creates a smaller inner triangle; spiral toward the center.
From pure straight lines, create the illusion of a spiral vortex.
steps_zh: 從一個三角形（或任何近正多邊形）開始。
從每個頂點向相鄰邊上的某一點畫直線——但不畫到底，留一段距離繼續往內走。
每一條新線在內部創造一個更小的三角形；持續向中心螺旋。
由純直線製造出「螺旋漩渦」的視覺幻覺。
why_important_en: Paradox is one of the most visually striking tangles — a classic example of 'straight lines creating curved illusion.'
why_important_zh: Paradox 是最具視覺衝擊的圖樣之一——「直線製造曲線錯覺」的經典範例。

steps_en: Draw two parallel curves to form a ribbon.
Inside the ribbon, draw continuous diagonal weaves — alternating thick and thin strands.
The strands interlace like bamboo weaving.
Add deeper shadows to the 'underlying' strands.
steps_zh: 畫兩條平行曲線形成一條帶。
帶內畫連續的對角編織——粗細交替的緞帶。
緞帶交織如竹編。
在「下層」緞帶加深陰影。
why_important_en: Munchin brings a strong 'woven' texture, often filled into Hollibaugh-style ribbons.
why_important_zh: Munchin 帶來強烈的「編織」質感，常被填入 Hollibaugh 式的長帶內。

creator_role: Co-founders of Zentangle (each has their own version)
steps_en: Draw a flame-like curving leaf.
Add a curving vein along the leaf's long axis.
Cluster many such leaves on a stem, varying the curl.
Vary sizes; let some leaves 'hide' behind others.
steps_zh: 畫一片像火焰般彎曲的葉。
沿葉長軸加一條彎曲的葉脈。
在莖上聚集多片這樣的葉，每片捲度不一。
大小有變化；讓部分葉「藏在」其他葉後面。
why_important_en: Flux is the workhorse for 'leaves, feathers, flowing edges.' Maria's and Rick's versions differ slightly in leaf shape.
why_important_zh: Flux 是「葉、羽、流動邊」的工作主力。共有 Maria 版與 Rick 版，葉形略有差異。

creator_role: Co-founder of Zentangle (named after Chillon Castle, Switzerland)
steps_en: Lay a square grid.
In each cell, draw a thick-armed cross with a small square at the center.
Connect the cross arms of adjacent cells, forming a continuous Greek-key pattern.
Fill empty spaces with black.
steps_zh: 鋪一格方格網。
每格內畫一個粗臂十字，中央留一小方塊。
把相鄰方格的十字臂連接起來，形成連續的「希臘回紋」。
把空格填黑。
why_important_en: Chillon transforms a simple grid into a labyrinthine, ancient-architecture-like pattern.
why_important_zh: Chillon 把簡單網格變成迷宮般、像古建築外牆的圖樣。

first_published_in: Official Zentangle Diva Dance video lessons
steps_en: Draw a long, asymmetric S-curve — blunt at one end, fine and curling at the other.
Draw another S-curve next to it, hugging the first.
Keep stacking; each curve echoes and pushes the previous one.
The whole resembles long leaves in wind, or dancing flames.
steps_zh: 畫一條長且不對稱的 S 形——一端粗鈍、另一端細捲。
旁邊再畫一條 S 形，靠著前一條鋪。
不斷堆疊；每一條都呼應、推移前一條。
整體像隨風飄動的長葉，或一團跳動的火苗。
why_important_en: Diva Dance is meditation-as-flow — beloved by practitioners, perfect for filling large empty spaces.
why_important_zh: Diva Dance 是「連續流動」的冥想——廣受喜愛，最適合填大塊空白。

steps_en: Draw a rounded form (a slightly flattened ball).
Inside, draw a series of parallel half-moon arcs all biased to one side.
It looks like a rolling, segmented pearl.
String many Bunzo along a stem.
steps_zh: 畫一個圓潤的形（略扁的球）。
內部畫一連串平行的半月弧，全部偏向同一邊。
看起來像一顆滾動、分節的珍珠。
沿莖串連許多 Bunzo。
why_important_en: The nested arcs make a string of Bunzo look like a chain of beads.
why_important_zh: 巢套的弧線讓整串看起來像一條珠鍊。

steps_en: Draw a square grid.
In each cell, draw diagonal lines from the four corners toward the center but stopping short — leaving a small square in the middle.
Fill two opposite triangles black, leaving the other two white.
The whole reads as a tessellation of 3D cubes.
steps_zh: 畫一格方格網。
每格內從四個角畫向中心的對角線，但不到中心——留下一個小方塊。
把對角的兩個三角填黑，另兩個留白。
整片讀起來像 3D 立方體的鑲嵌。
why_important_en: Cubine is the simplest tangle that produces a strong 3D illusion.
why_important_zh: Cubine 是最簡單就能呈現「立體錯覺」的圖樣。

first_published_in: Official Zentangle method; Zentangle Primer Vol. 1
steps_en: This is the official concept of the 'pencil string' that divides a tile into zones.
Lightly draw a free curve in pencil — a simple Z, or a meandering doodle — splitting the square into 3-6 areas.
Each zone becomes the canvas for one tangle.
steps_zh: 這是「鉛筆 string」的官方概念，用以將紙磚分區。
輕輕用鉛筆在紙磚上畫一條自由曲線——可以是簡單的 Z、也可以是蜿蜒的塗鴉——把方形分成 3–6 個區塊。
每個區塊就是一個圖樣的畫布。
why_important_en: String is the soul of Zentangle: it provides structure without dictating outcome.
why_important_zh: String 是禪繞的靈魂：它建立結構，卻不規定結果。

creator_role: Author of 'Yoga for Your Brain' (variation of Static)
steps_en: A variation of Static with extra zingy flair — see Static base steps and add curl details.
steps_zh: Static 的變體，加上更多彈跳裝飾——參考 Static 基本步驟並加上小捲。
why_important_en: Demonstrates how a base tangle can spawn variations.
why_important_zh: 示範一個基礎圖樣如何衍生出多種變體。

first_published_in: Yoga for Your Brain; later TanglePatterns.com
steps_en: Draw a vertical column of stacked 'W' shapes.
Draw another column of W's offset by half a W to interlock.
Both columns weave.
Fill the small interior diamonds with black.
steps_zh: 畫一直行相疊的「W」形。
旁邊再畫一直行 W 形，與前一行錯開半個 W，使之互鎖。
兩行交織。
中間夾出的小菱形填黑。
why_important_en: W2 creates a strong woven feeling from a single letter shape.
why_important_zh: W2 用單一字母製造強烈的編織感。

steps_en: Draw parallel lines with varying thickness and spacing.
Optionally let the lines curve to follow the area's contour.
Group three to five lines with alternating thicknesses.
steps_zh: 在區塊內畫粗細與間距變化的平行線。
視需要讓線條跟隨區塊輪廓彎曲。
三到五條為一組，粗細交錯。
why_important_en: Striping is among the simplest tangles; as a calm filler, it balances busier neighbors.
why_important_zh: Striping 是最簡單的圖樣之一；作為冷靜填料，可平衡熱鬧的鄰居。

creator_role: Author of 'Yoga for Your Brain' (Zander = her son's name)
steps_en: Draw a continuous zigzag line — alternating peaks and valleys.
Draw another zigzag line beside it, mirrored, with diamonds forming between.
Add diagonal lines or fill the diamonds with black.
steps_zh: 在區塊上畫一條連續的鋸齒線——尖峰與尖谷交替。
旁邊再畫一條鋸齒線，與前一條鏡射，中間形成一排菱形。
加斜線或把菱形填黑。
why_important_en: Zig zag is the dynamic counterpart to Striping.
why_important_zh: Zig zag 是 Striping 的「動感版」對照。

steps_en: Draw a long curving stem.
Shoot pairs of pointed leaves like palm fronds from both sides.
Each leaf is a long teardrop; alternate left/right along the stem.
Fill alternating leaves with diagonal hatching or solid black.
steps_zh: 畫一條彎曲的長莖。
從莖兩側射出一對對尖葉，像棕櫚葉。
每片葉是長水滴形；沿莖左右交錯。
把交替的葉片以斜線或實心黑填滿。
why_important_en: Nipa adds tropical, fern-like long texture, especially good for Phicops and Zendala.
why_important_zh: Nipa 帶來熱帶、蕨類般的長條質感，特別適合 Phicops 與 Zendala。

steps_en: Draw a curving stem.
Shoot bundles of short straight lines from the stem — each bundle has 5-7 needles.
Each bundle radiates from a single point.
Stagger bundles along the stem.
steps_zh: 畫一條彎曲的莖。
從莖上射出一束束短直線——每束 5–7 根針。
每束從同一點放射開來。
沿莖錯開排列。
why_important_en: Verdigogh is the 'pine needle representative' for winter or evergreen-themed tiles.
why_important_zh: Verdigogh 是冬季或常綠主題紙磚的「松針代表」。

steps_en: Draw a hexagon.
Inside, draw a slightly smaller hexagon.
Tile the area with hexagonal honeycomb.
Add a small cross, dot, or arc inside each hexagon — like turtle-shell markings.
steps_zh: 畫一個六角形。
內部再畫一個略小的六角形。
將整片區塊以六角形蜂巢鑲嵌。
每個六角形內加上一個小十字、點、或弧——像龜殼上的紋路。
why_important_en: Tortuca is the basic honeycomb texture — also reminiscent of Taiwanese traditional cake molds.
why_important_zh: Tortuca 是蜂巢質感的基礎圖樣——也是台灣常見的傳統蛋糕模子上的紋樣。

steps_en: Auras is technically a 'technique' rather than a single tangle.
Outside any shape, draw a thin offset contour line (the aura).
Draw a second, third aura.
Auras can be uniform or tapered, and can also be drawn 'inward,' contracting toward the center.
steps_zh: Auras 嚴格來說是個「技法」，不是單一圖樣。
在任何形狀外側畫一條細的偏移輪廓線（光環）；再畫第二、第三道。
光環可以等距、可以漸變。
也可以「往內」畫，向中心收縮。
why_important_en: Auras is the most universal tool in the Zentangle vocabulary — turning any single line into a 'glow' or 'echo.'
why_important_zh: Auras 是禪繞詞彙中最萬用的工具——把單一線條變成「光暈」「回聲」的感覺。

steps_en: Sparkle is the technique of intentionally leaving an unbroken white line within a pattern to suggest highlight.
When drawing a long shape (a leaf, a curl, an aura), lift the pen briefly, skip 1-2mm, and continue — the resulting white line is the highlight.
steps_zh: Sparkle 是「在圖樣中刻意留下一條未斷白線」以暗示高光的技法。
在描繪長形物（一片葉、一個捲、一道光環）時，提筆瞬間，跳過 1–2 mm 再續筆，留下的白線就是光點。
why_important_en: Sparkle is the cheapest, most powerful 3D effect — like the bright reflection on the surface of tea.
why_important_zh: Sparkle 是最便宜、效果最強的立體感技巧——像茶湯表面那一道反光。

steps_en: Draw a small circle in the center.
Radiate even number of rays outward (8, 12, or 16).
Between each pair of adjacent rays, draw a triangular wedge that slightly overlaps the next.
Continue adding outer rings of smaller wedges.
steps_zh: 中央畫一個小圓。
從圓向外射出偶數條（8、12、16）直線輻射。
每對相鄰輻射之間畫一個三角楔，輕微疊到下一個楔。
沿輻射方向往外加一圈又一圈更小的楔。
why_important_en: Arukas is one of the most popular advanced tangles — symmetrical, hypnotic, and deeply satisfying to complete.
why_important_zh: Arukas 是最受歡迎的進階圖樣之一——對稱、催眠般的視覺效果，畫起來特別有成就感。

steps_en: Draw a square grid.
Place a solid dot in every other cell.
In remaining cells, draw a pair of crossing diagonals.
Read as a chessboard of dots and X's.
steps_zh: 畫一格方格網。
每隔一格放一個實心圓點。
其餘格內畫一對交叉的對角線。
整片讀起來像「點」與「X」的棋盤。
why_important_en: Rixty is a clean geometric filler suited for backgrounds.
why_important_zh: Rixty 是乾淨幾何的填料，適合做背景。

steps_en: Draw a vertical line.
Shoot a series of thin upward-J strokes from it, evenly spaced.
Draw another vertical line beside it with another row of J-strokes facing the opposite direction.
The two columns visually interlock.
steps_zh: 畫一條垂直線。
從它射出一系列細的向上 J 形筆劃，間距均勻。
旁邊再畫另一條垂直線與另一組 J 形筆劃，方向相反。
兩行視覺上互鎖。
why_important_en: Quib gives vertical rhythm — lighter than Striping.
why_important_zh: Quib 給垂直節奏——比 Striping 更輕巧。

steps_en: Sketch a square in pencil.
Starting at one corner, draw a continuous line spiraling inward — turning 90° at each step like a wall-less labyrinth.
The line ends at the center of the square.
Repeat in adjacent squares; alternate starting corners so spirals interlock.
steps_zh: 鉛筆畫一個方形。
從一個角開始，畫一條連續的線往內螺旋——每一步轉 90°，像沒有牆的迷宮。
線終結在方形中央。
在相鄰方形重複；交錯起點，使螺旋互鎖。
why_important_en: Amaze is the classic 'maze' tangle, deeply meditative when drawn slowly.
why_important_zh: Amaze 是經典的「迷宮」圖樣，慢慢畫特別療癒。

steps_en: Draw a row of vertical wavy lines, like cross-sections of stacked planks.
Inside each plank, draw nested ovals — like wood grain rings.
Optionally darken one edge of each plank.
steps_zh: 畫一排垂直波浪線，像層層木板的剖面。
在每塊木板內畫巢套的橢圓——像年輪木紋。
視需要把每塊木板的一邊加深。
why_important_en: Wud is the go-to for any 'wood / bark / texture' effect — especially for Taiwanese readers' familiarity with hinoki cypress.
why_important_zh: Wud 是任何「木質、樹皮、紋理」效果的首選——特別適合台灣讀者熟悉的檜木質感聯想。
